content_en:
2) The Prohibition Of Eating In A Reclining Position Or When Lying Prone On One's Stomach

Abu Juhaifah # said, “I was with the Messenger of Allah when he said to a man that was with him, ‘I do not eat while I am reclining (or leaning).’” Ibn Hajr pointed out that scholars disagree about what is meant by reclining or leaning. Some say it means to sit down in a comfortable position; others say it means to lean on one’s right or left side; and yet others say it means to rest one’s weight on one’s left hand, which is fixed firmly on the ground. This last opinion is perhaps based on a narration that has a weak chain and is related by Ibn ‘Adee. In it, the Prophet explained that a man should not lean on his left hand while he is eating. Maalik said about resting one’s weight on one’s left hand, “Tt is a form of leaning.” And commenting on this saying of Maalik, Ibn Hajr said, “This shows that Maalik disliked for one to eat in any position that might be construed as leaning or reclining, meaning that, in his view, there isn’t a specific form of leaning that is disliked (but that rather all forms of leaning are disliked).””

Perhaps the reason why it is disliked to eat while one is in a reclining position is that tyrants and kings from foreign lands

Chapter - 9 The Manners Of Eating And Drinking 195

would eat in that position; furthermore, it is the position of one who is saying, in his posture and not with his words, that he wants to eat a lot of food. Ibn Hajr said, ‘If it is established that it is disliked or contrary to what is better to eat in a reclining position, it is recommended that one who is eating sit on his knees, with his feet facing up, or with his right leg up (with his right knee pointing upwards) while he is sitting on his left leg.”

The second prohibited posture during eating is for one to lie prone on one’s stomach. Ibn ‘Umar & said, “The Messenger of Allah 2% forbade two [practices related to] eating: [he forbade] sitting at a table on which alcohol is imbibed, and [he forbade] eating while one is lying prone on his stomach.)

Related Issue: How did the Prophet # sit down during a meal? When the Prophet # would eat, he would sit in the Maq’ee position (to sit on one’s buttocks, with one’s feet erect underneath). It is also related that he would sit down during a meal on his knees, and the bottom of his left foot would be resting against the top of his right foot, a Position that showed his humility and submission to his Lord 3¢./7!

pits ly WS he lis a bp

' Related by Abu Daawood (3774), and Al-Albaanee ruled that Abu Daawood’s narration of the hadith is authentic. Ibn Maajah related it as

"I Ton Al-Qayyim related this in Za’ad Al-Ma’aad (4/221)

“Verily, Allah 8 has made me a noble (and good-natured) slave, and He 8 did not make me an obstinate tyrant")

3) When It Is Time For prayer And When Food Is Being Served, Eat First And Then Pray

Anas #4 reported that the Prophet # said, Glbdy L220 lsc

“When dinner is laid down and the prayer Ugeemat (i.e., the Igaamah is made and the prayer begins), then begin with dinner.’

Ibn ‘Umar & reported that the Messenger of Allah #& said,

as Vg clad, yes San casty dust lke B55 bp es A

“When the dinner of one of you is laid down and the prayer Ugeemat (i.e., the Igaamah is made and the prayer begins), then he should begin with dinner. And let him not rush, [but instead wait] until he finishes it (his dinner),

Whenever dinner would be served to him at the time of prayer, Ibn ‘Umar would not leave to Pray until he finished his meal. Naafai’ related that Ibn ‘Umar % would send him [to bring food] when he was fasting; food would be served to him while the call was being made for the Maghrib prayer. Then the prayer would commence. Ibn ‘Umar 4 would hear that it had commenced, but he would not leave his dinner, nor would he rush; instead, he would wait to finish his dinner. Then he would go out and pray.

"1 Tbn Maajah related it (3263), and the wording mentioned above is from his narration of the hadith; Ibn Hajr gave a Hasan grade to its chain, and about the narration, Al-Albaanee said, ““sahih.” (2658). Except for a small portion, Abu Daawood related it as well (3773).

"1 Related by Bukhaaree (674), Muslim (559), Ahmad (5772), At-Tirmidhee (354), Abu Daawood (3757), Ibn Maajah (934), and Ad-Daarimee (1281).

'8] Related by Bukhaaree (674), Muslim (559), Ahmad (5772), At-Tirmidhee (354), Abu Daawood (3757), Ibn Maajah (934), and Ad-Daarimee (1281).

Chapter - 9 The Manners Of Eating And Drinking 197

Naafai’ then related that Ibn ‘Umar would say, “The Prophet of Allah #¢ said,

“Do not rush from your dinner when it is served to you.”

You should eat first, so that, during prayer, your mind will not be preoccupied with food, and so that you can concentrate on your prayer.

Once, when Abu Hurairah # and Ibn ‘Abbaas were eating and there was roasted meat in the oven, the Muaddhin (Caller to prayer) wanted to make Iqaamah (to commence the prayer). Ibn ‘Abbaas & said to him, “Do not rush, so that we do not stand with something in ourselves [being preoccupied about the meal].”/2] The ruling in this issue is not limited to dinner alone, but rather applies to any meal that a person looks forward to having. Another prohibition supports this ruling: the Prophet # forbade a person to pray when food is served and when one is fighting the urge to relieve himself (to defecate or urinate). ‘Aaisha said, “I heard the Messenger of Allah #% say,

‘There is no prayer when food arrives, nor when one is pushing back Al-Akhbathaan (urine and stool).’")

Related Issue: Some scholars have said, “If food is being served when the [congregational] prayer commences, then one should eat a few morsels to cut off the intensity of his hunger [, and then he should immediately go and pray].” An-Nawawee refuted this view, saying, “And the Prophet's saying,

“And let him not rush, but instead [wait] until he finishes it

" Related by Ahmad (6323).

"I Tbn Hajr related this in Fathul-Baaree (2/189). He said that Sa’eed Ibn Mansoor and Ibn Abee Shaibah related it with a Hasan chain.

1 Related by Muslim (560), Ahmad (23646), and Abu Daawood (89).

proves that one should continue to eat until he completely satisfies his appetite. This is the correct view, and as for what some of our companions have interpreted - that one should eat only a few morsels to break the intensity of hunger - then that is not correct. In fact, the above-mentioned hadith clearly disproves that view.”

Question: When food is served and the [congregational] prayer commences, is it compulsory for one to eat, based on the apparent wording of the hadith? Or does the hadith convey a meaning of ‘recommended’?

Answer: The action of Ibn ‘Umar , as related by Ahmad and others, proves that precedence should always be given to one’s meal over prayer; some scholars, however, have said that the ruling depends on the individual - how intense his desire is to eat. So if a person strongly desires to eat, then it is better in his case to eat and then pray, so that he goes to prayer with a heart that is free to concentrate on worship. A saying of Abu AdDardaa # expresses that meaning: “It is from the [sound religious] understanding of a person to do what he needs to do [first], so that he goes to prayer with a heart that is unoccupied.” I feel that the strongest opinion in this issue is the one expressed by Al-Haafidh Ibn Hajr, who said that various narrations point to the intensity of one’s desire to eat as being the reason why one should eat first and then pray. So the ruling in this issue revolves on the reason why it was legislated: if the reason is applicable to a person, the ruling applies; otherwise, it doesn’t. Or in other words, if one wants to eat and fears that he will be thinking about food during prayer, he should eat first; but if he isn’t all that hungry and knows that he will not be thinking about food during prayer, he does not have to eat first.

"] Bukhaaree related it Ta’leeqan in “The Book Of Aadhaan.””

Chapter - 9 The Manners Of Eating And Drinking 199

can provide solid grounds for the legislation of washing one’s hands before one eats. Al-Baihagee said, “The hadith about washing one’s hands after eating is Hasan (acceptable), but no hadith is established about washing one’s hands before eating.”

Nonetheless, it is recommended to wash your hands before eating in order to remove any filth or impurities that might do harm to your health. And about the ruling for washing hands before a meal, there are two conflicting opinions related from Imam Ahmad - Karaaha (that it is disliked to do so) and Istihbaab (that it is recommended to do so). Imam Maalik elaborated, saying that the legislation of washing one’s hands before eating is limited to when there is any filth or impurity on one’s hands. In his Aadaab, Ibn Muflih took the stance that it is recommended to wash one’s hands before eating, and his view is shared by a group of scholars. Nonetheless, there is latitude in this issue, and all praise is to Allah, Lord of all that exists.

There are, on the other hand, authentic narrations related about washing one’s hands after eating. For example, Abu Hurairah reported that the Messenger of Allah #% said,
content_id:
2) Larangan Makan Sambil Bersandar atau Tengkurap

Abu Juhaifah # berkata, "Aku bersama Rasulullah ketika beliau berkata kepada seorang lelaki yang ada bersamanya, 'Aku tidak makan sambil bersandar (atau berpegangan).'" Ibn Hajr menunjukkan bahwa para ulama berbeda pendapat tentang apa yang dimaksud dengan bersandar atau berpegangan. Sebagian mengatakan artinya duduk dalam posisi yang nyaman; yang lain mengatakan artinya bersandar ke sisi kanan atau kiri; dan yang lain lagi mengatakan artinya menopang berat badan pada tangan kiri yang bertumpu kuat di tanah. Pendapat terakhir ini mungkin didasarkan pada sebuah riwayat yang memiliki sanad lemah yang diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Adee. Di dalamnya, Nabi menjelaskan bahwa seseorang tidak boleh bersandar pada tangan kirinya saat makan. Maalik berkata tentang menopang berat badan pada tangan kiri, "Itu adalah salah satu bentuk bersandar." Dan mengomentari perkataan Maalik ini, Ibn Hajr berkata, "Ini menunjukkan bahwa Maalik tidak menyukai seseorang makan dalam posisi apa pun yang dapat ditafsirkan sebagai bersandar atau bertumpu, artinya, menurut pandangannya, tidak ada bentuk bersandar tertentu yang dimakruhkan (melainkan semua bentuk bersandar dimakruhkan).""

Barangkali alasan mengapa dimakruhkan makan sambil bersandar adalah bahwa para tiran dan raja dari negeri asing

Bab - 9 Adab Makan dan Minum 195

biasa makan dalam posisi tersebut; lebih jauh lagi, itu adalah posisi seseorang yang berkata, melalui sikapnya dan bukan dengan kata-katanya, bahwa ia ingin makan dalam jumlah banyak. Ibn Hajr berkata, "Jika telah ditetapkan bahwa dimakruhkan atau bertentangan dengan yang lebih baik untuk makan dalam posisi bersandar, maka dianjurkan bagi orang yang makan untuk duduk di atas lututnya, dengan kaki menghadap ke atas, atau dengan kaki kanan terangkat (dengan lutut kanan mengarah ke atas) sementara ia duduk di atas kaki kirinya."

Postur kedua yang dilarang saat makan adalah tengkurap. Ibn 'Umar & berkata, "Rasulullah 2% melarang dua [praktik terkait] makan: [beliau melarang] duduk di meja yang di atasnya diminum khamr, dan [beliau melarang] makan sambil tengkurap."

Masalah Terkait: Bagaimana Nabi # duduk saat makan? Ketika Nabi # makan, beliau duduk dalam posisi Maq'ee (duduk di atas bokong, dengan kaki tegak di bawahnya). Diriwayatkan pula bahwa beliau duduk saat makan di atas lututnya, dan bagian bawah kaki kirinya beristirahat di atas punggung kaki kanannya, suatu posisi yang menunjukkan kerendahan hati dan ketundukannya kepada Rabb-nya 3¢./7!

pits ly WS he lis a bp

' Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Daawood (3774), dan Al-Albaanee memutuskan bahwa riwayat Abu Daawood tentang hadith ini adalah sahih. Ibn Maajah meriwayatkannya sebagai

"I Ton Al-Qayyim meriwayatkan ini dalam Za'ad Al-Ma'aad (4/221)

"Sesungguhnya, Allah 8 telah menjadikanku seorang hamba yang mulia (dan berperangai baik), dan Dia 8 tidak menjadikanku seorang tiran yang keras kepala")

3) Ketika Tiba Waktu Salah dan Ketika Makanan Dihidangkan, Makanlah Dahulu Kemudian Salat

Anas #4 melaporkan bahwa Nabi # bersabda, Glbdy L220 lsc

"Apabila makan malam telah dihidangkan dan salat di-iqamatkan (yakni, Iqaamah dikumandangkan dan salat dimulai), maka mulailah dengan makan malam."

Ibn 'Umar & melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah #& bersabda,

as Vg clad, yes San casty dust lke B55 bp es A

"Apabila makan malam salah seorang dari kalian dihidangkan dan salat di-iqamatkan (yakni, Iqaamah dikumandangkan dan salat dimulai), maka hendaklah ia mulai dengan makan malam. Dan janganlah ia tergesa-gesa, [melainkan tunggulah] hingga ia menyelesaikannya (makan malamnya)."

Setiap kali makan malam dihidangkan pada waktu salat, Ibn 'Umar tidak pergi untuk salat hingga ia menyelesaikan makannya. Naafai' meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn 'Umar % mengutusnya [untuk membawa makanan] ketika ia sedang berpuasa; makanan dihidangkan kepadanya sementara azan Maghrib sedang dikumandangkan. Kemudian salat pun dimulai. Ibn 'Umar 4 mendengar bahwa salat telah dimulai, tetapi ia tidak meninggalkan makan malamnya, dan tidak pula tergesa-gesa; sebaliknya, ia menunggu untuk menyelesaikan makan malamnya. Kemudian ia keluar dan salat.

"1 Ibn Maajah meriwayatkannya (3263), dan redaksi yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayatnya tentang hadith; Ibn Hajr memberikan penilaian Hasan pada sanadnya, dan mengenai riwayat tersebut, Al-Albaanee berkata, "sahih." (2658). Kecuali sebagian kecil, Abu Daawood juga meriwayatkannya (3773).

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (674), Muslim (559), Ahmad (5772), At-Tirmidhee (354), Abu Daawood (3757), Ibn Maajah (934), dan Ad-Daarimee (1281).

'8] Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (674), Muslim (559), Ahmad (5772), At-Tirmidhee (354), Abu Daawood (3757), Ibn Maajah (934), dan Ad-Daarimee (1281).

Bab - 9 Adab Makan dan Minum 197

Naafai' kemudian meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn 'Umar berkata, "Nabi Allah #¢ bersabda,

"Janganlah tergesa-gesa meninggalkan makan malammu ketika dihidangkan kepadamu."

Hendaklah engkau makan dahulu, agar saat salat pikiranmu tidak disibukkan dengan makanan, dan agar engkau dapat berkonsentrasi dalam salatmu.

Suatu ketika, ketika Abu Hurairah # dan Ibn 'Abbaas sedang makan dan ada daging panggang di dalam oven, Muadzdzan (Juru azan) ingin mengumandangkan Iqaamah (untuk memulai salat). Ibn 'Abbaas & berkata kepadanya, "Jangan tergesa-gesa, agar kita tidak berdiri dengan sesuatu dalam diri kita [yang menyibukkan pikiran tentang makanan]."/2] Hukum dalam masalah ini tidak terbatas pada makan malam saja, melainkan berlaku untuk makanan apa pun yang dinantikan seseorang. Larangan lain mendukung hukum ini: Nabi # melarang seseorang untuk salat ketika makanan dihidangkan dan ketika seseorang menahan keinginan untuk buang hajat (buang air besar atau kecil). 'Aaisha berkata, "Aku mendengar Rasulullah #% bersabda,

'Tidak ada salat ketika makanan tiba, dan tidak pula ketika seseorang menahan Al-Akhbathaan (air kencing dan tinja).'")

Masalah Terkait: Sebagian ulama berkata, "Jika makanan sedang dihidangkan ketika salat [berjamaah] dimulai, maka hendaklah ia makan beberapa suap untuk memutus intensitas rasa laparnya [, kemudian ia harus segera pergi salat]." An-Nawawee membantah pandangan ini, seraya berkata, "Dan sabda Nabi,

"Dan janganlah ia tergesa-gesa, melainkan [tunggulah] hingga ia menyelesaikannya

" Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad (6323).

"I Ibn Hajr meriwayatkan ini dalam Fathul-Baaree (2/189). Beliau berkata bahwa Sa'eed Ibn Mansoor dan Ibn Abee Shaibah meriwayatkannya dengan sanad Hasan.

1 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (560), Ahmad (23646), dan Abu Daawood (89).

membuktikan bahwa seseorang harus terus makan hingga ia benar-benar memuaskan nafsu makannya. Inilah pandangan yang benar, dan adapun apa yang ditafsirkan oleh sebagian sahabat kami - bahwa seseorang hanya perlu makan beberapa suap untuk memecah intensitas rasa lapar - maka itu tidaklah benar. Bahkan, hadith yang disebutkan di atas jelas membantah pandangan tersebut."

Pertanyaan: Ketika makanan dihidangkan dan salat [berjamaah] dimulai, apakah wajib bagi seseorang untuk makan, berdasarkan redaksi zahir hadith? Ataukah hadith tersebut menyampaikan makna 'dianjurkan'?

Jawaban: Perbuatan Ibn 'Umar , sebagaimana diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad dan lainnya, membuktikan bahwa hendaknya selalu mendahulukan makan daripada salat; namun sebagian ulama berpendapat bahwa hukumnya bergantung pada individu - seberapa kuat keinginannya untuk makan. Jadi jika seseorang sangat ingin makan, maka lebih baik baginya untuk makan dahulu kemudian salat, agar ia pergi salat dengan hati yang bebas untuk berkonsentrasi pada ibadah. Sebuah perkataan Abu AdDardaa # mengungkapkan makna tersebut: "Termasuk dari [pemahaman agama yang baik] seseorang adalah melakukan apa yang ia butuhkan [terlebih dahulu], agar ia pergi salat dengan hati yang tidak tersibukkan." Saya merasa bahwa pendapat terkuat dalam masalah ini adalah yang diungkapkan oleh Al-Haafidh Ibn Hajr, yang mengatakan bahwa berbagai riwayat menunjukkan intensitas keinginan seseorang untuk makan sebagai alasan mengapa seseorang harus makan dahulu kemudian salat. Jadi hukum dalam masalah ini berputar pada alasan mengapa ia disyariatkan: jika alasannya berlaku bagi seseorang, maka hukumnya berlaku; jika tidak, maka tidak. Atau dengan kata lain, jika seseorang ingin makan dan khawatir akan memikirkan makanan selama salat, hendaklah ia makan dahulu; tetapi jika ia tidak terlalu lapar dan tahu bahwa ia tidak akan memikirkan makanan selama salat, ia tidak harus makan dahulu.

"] Bukhaaree meriwayatkannya secara Ta'leeqan dalam "Kitab Azan.""

Bab - 9 Adab Makan dan Minum 199

dapat memberikan landasan yang kuat bagi syariat mencuci tangan sebelum makan. Al-Baihagee berkata, "Hadith tentang mencuci tangan setelah makan adalah Hasan (dapat diterima), tetapi tidak ada hadith yang sahih tentang mencuci tangan sebelum makan."

Meskipun demikian, dianjurkan untuk mencuci tangan sebelum makan guna menghilangkan kotoran atau najis yang mungkin membahayakan kesehatan. Mengenai hukum mencuci tangan sebelum makan, terdapat dua pendapat yang berbeda yang diriwayatkan dari Imam Ahmad — Karaaha (bahwa hal itu dimakruhkan) dan Istihbaab (bahwa hal itu dianjurkan). Imam Maalik menjelaskan bahwa pensyariatan mencuci tangan sebelum makan terbatas pada kondisi ketika terdapat kotoran atau najis pada tangan seseorang. Dalam kitab Aadaab-nya, Ibn Muflih berpendapat bahwa dianjurkan untuk mencuci tangan sebelum makan, dan pandangan ini juga dipegang oleh sekelompok ulama. Meskipun demikian, terdapat keluasan dalam masalah ini, dan segala puji bagi Allah, Rabb semesta alam.

Di sisi lain, terdapat riwayat-riwayat sahih yang berkaitan dengan mencuci tangan setelah makan. Sebagai contoh, Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,
content_ar: 2) The Prohibition Of Eating In A Reclining Position Or عندما Lying Prone On One's Stomach Abu Juhaifah # said, “I was with الرسول ﷺ of الله عندما قال to a man that was with him, ‘I do not eat بينما I am reclining (or leaning).’” Ibn Hajr pointed out that العلماء disagree about what is meant by reclining or leaning. Some say it means to sit down in a comfortable position; others say it means to lean on one’s الحق or left side; and yet others say it means to rest one’s weight on one’s left hand, which is fixed firmly on the ground. This last opinion is perhaps based on a narration that has a weak chain and is related by Ibn ‘Adee. In it, النبي ﷺ explained that a man should not lean on his left hand بينما he is eating. Maalik said about resting one’s weight on one’s left hand, “Tt is a form of leaning.” And commenting on this saying of Maalik, Ibn Hajr said, “This shows that Maalik disliked for one to eat in any position that might be construed as leaning or reclining, meaning that, in his view, there isn’t a specific form of leaning that is disliked (but that rather all forms of leaning are disliked).”” Perhaps the العقل why يُكره to eat بينما one is in a reclining position is that tyrants and kings from foreign lands الباب - 9 The الآداب Of Eating And Drinking 195 would eat in that position; علاوة على ذلك, it is the position of one who is saying, in his posture and not with his words, that he wants to eat a lot of food. Ibn Hajr said, ‘إذا ثبت that يُكره or contrary to what is better to eat in a reclining position, يستحب that one who is eating sit on his knees, with his feet facing up, or with his الحق leg up (with his الحق knee pointing upwards) بينما he is sitting on his left leg.” The ثانياً prohibited posture خلال eating is for one to lie prone on one’s stomach. Ibn ‘عمر & said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% forbade two [practices related to] eating: [he forbade] sitting at a table on which alcohol is imbibed, and [he forbade] eating بينما one is lying prone on his stomach.) Related Issue: How did النبي ﷺ # sit down خلال a meal? عندما النبي ﷺ # would eat, he would sit in the Maq’ee position (to sit on one’s buttocks, with one’s feet erect underneath). It is أيضاً related that he would sit down خلال a meal on his knees, and the bottom of his left foot would be resting against the top of his الحق foot, a Position that showed his التواضع and submission to his Lord 3¢./7! pits ly WS he lis a bp ' Related by Abu Daawood (3774), and Al-Albaanee ruled that Abu Daawood’s narration of the الحديث is authentic. Ibn Maajah related it as "I Ton Al-Qayyim related this in Za’ad Al-Ma’aad (4/221) “Verily, الله 8 has made me a noble (and الخير-natured) slave, and He 8 did not make me an obstinate tyrant") 3) عندما It Is Time For الصلاة And عندما Food Is Being Served, Eat أولاً And Then Pray أنس #4 reported that النبي ﷺ # said, Glbdy L220 lsc “عندما dinner is laid down and the الصلاة Ugeemat (i.e., the Igaamah is made and the الصلاة begins), then begin with dinner.’ Ibn ‘عمر & reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #& said, as Vg clad, yes San casty dust lke B55 bp es A “عندما the dinner of one of you is laid down and the الصلاة Ugeemat (i.e., the Igaamah is made and the الصلاة begins), then he should begin with dinner. And let him not rush, [but instead wait] حتى he finishes it (his dinner), كلما dinner would be served to him at the time of الصلاة, Ibn ‘عمر would not leave to Pray حتى he finished his meal. Naafai’ related that Ibn ‘عمر % would send him [to bring food] عندما he was الصيام; food would be served to him بينما the call was being made for the Maghrib الصلاة. Then the الصلاة would commence. Ibn ‘عمر 4 would hear that it had commenced, but he would not leave his dinner, nor would he rush; instead, he would wait to finish his dinner. Then he would go out and pray. "1 Tbn Maajah related it (3263), and the wording mentioned above is from his narration of the الحديث; Ibn Hajr gave a حسن grade to its chain, and about the narration, Al-Albaanee said, ““صحيح.” (2658). Except for a small portion, Abu Daawood related it as well (3773). "1 Related by Bukhaaree (674), مسلم (559), أحمد (5772), At-Tirmidhee (354), Abu Daawood (3757), Ibn Maajah (934), and Ad-Daarimee (1281). '8] Related by Bukhaaree (674), مسلم (559), أحمد (5772), At-Tirmidhee (354), Abu Daawood (3757), Ibn Maajah (934), and Ad-Daarimee (1281). الباب - 9 The الآداب Of Eating And Drinking 197 Naafai’ then related that Ibn ‘عمر would say, “النبي ﷺ of الله #¢ said, “Do not rush from your dinner عندما it is served to you.” You should eat أولاً, لذا that, خلال الصلاة, your العقل will not be preoccupied with food, and لذا that you can concentrate on your الصلاة. حين, عندما أبو هريرة # and Ibn ‘Abbaas were eating and there was roasted meat in the oven, the Muaddhin (Caller to الصلاة) wanted to make Iqaamah (to commence the الصلاة). Ibn ‘Abbaas & said to him, “Do not rush, لذا that we do not stand with something in ourselves [being preoccupied about the meal].”/2] The ruling in this issue is not limited to dinner alone, but rather applies to any meal that a person looks forward to having. Another prohibition supports this ruling: النبي ﷺ # forbade a person to pray عندما food is served and عندما one is fighting the urge to relieve himself (to defecate or urinate). ‘Aaisha said, “I heard الرسول ﷺ of الله #% say, ‘There is no الصلاة عندما food arrives, nor عندما one is pushing back Al-Akhbathaan (urine and stool).’") Related Issue: Some العلماء have said, “إذا food is being served عندما the [congregational] الصلاة commences, then ينبغي للمرء eat a few morsels to cut off the intensity of his hunger [, and then he should immediately go and pray].” An-Nawawee refuted this view, saying, “And النبي ﷺ's saying, “And let him not rush, but instead [wait] حتى he finishes it " Related by أحمد (6323). "I Tbn Hajr related this in Fathul-Baaree (2/189). قال that Sa’eed Ibn Mansoor and Ibn Abee Shaibah related it with a حسن chain. 1 Related by مسلم (560), أحمد (23646), and Abu Daawood (89). proves that ينبغي للمرء continue to eat حتى he completely satisfies his appetite. This is القول الراجح, and أما what some of our الصحابة have interpreted - that ينبغي للمرء eat only a few morsels to break the intensity of hunger - then that is not correct. In fact, the above-mentioned الحديث clearly disproves that view.” Question: عندما food is served and the [congregational] الصلاة commences, is it compulsory for one to eat, based on the apparent wording of the الحديث? Or does the الحديث convey a meaning of ‘recommended’? Answer: The action of Ibn ‘عمر , as related by أحمد and others, proves that precedence should always be given to one’s meal over الصلاة; some العلماء, غير أن, have said that the ruling depends on the individual - how intense his desire is to eat. لذا إذا a person strongly desires to eat, then it is better in his case to eat and then pray, لذا that he goes to الصلاة with a القلب that is free to concentrate on worship. A saying of Abu AdDardaa # expresses that meaning: “It is from the [sound religious] الفهم of a person to do what he needs to do [أولاً], لذا that he goes to الصلاة with a القلب that is unoccupied.” I feel that the strongest opinion in this issue is the one expressed by Al-Haafidh Ibn Hajr, who said that various narrations point to the intensity of one’s desire to eat as being the العقل why ينبغي للمرء eat أولاً and then pray. لذا the ruling in this issue revolves on the العقل why it was legislated: إذا the العقل is applicable to a person, the ruling applies; otherwise, it doesn’t. Or بعبارة أخرى, إذا one wants to eat and fears that he will be thinking about food خلال الصلاة, he should eat أولاً; but إذا he isn’t all that hungry and knows that he will not be thinking about food خلال الصلاة, he does not have to eat أولاً. "] Bukhaaree related it Ta’leeqan in “The الكتاب Of Aadhaan.”” الباب - 9 The الآداب Of Eating And Drinking 199 can provide solid grounds for the legislation of washing one’s hands قبل one eats. Al-Baihagee said, “The الحديث about washing one’s hands بعد eating is حسن (acceptable), but no الحديث is established about washing one’s hands قبل eating.” Nonetheless, يستحب to wash your hands قبل eating من أجل remove any filth or impurities that might do harm to your الصحة. And about the ruling for washing hands قبل a meal, there are two conflicting opinions related from الإمام أحمد - Karaaha (that يُكره to do لذا) and Istihbaab (that يستحب to do لذا). الإمام Maalik elaborated, saying that the legislation of washing one’s hands قبل eating is limited to عندما there is any filth or impurity on one’s hands. In his Aadaab, Ibn Muflih took the stance that يستحب to wash one’s hands قبل eating, and his view is shared by a group of العلماء. Nonetheless, there is latitude in this issue, and all praise is to الله, Lord of all that exists. There are, من ناحية أخرى, authentic narrations related about washing one’s hands بعد eating. على سبيل المثال, أبو هريرة reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said,
